notes: ENISA produces authoritative EU cybersecurity guidance including ML threat taxonomies and AI security frameworks.

data.description: Taxonomy of threats to machine learning algorithms, published by ENISA as part of EU cybersecurity guidance.
data.notes: Covers threats across the ML lifecycle including evasion, poisoning, and model extraction. Published as a formal ENISA publication. Threat categories are described by slug names — no formal per-item identifiers.

versions_available.note: Initial ENISA ML threat taxonomy publication.

data.note: Conceptual taxonomy — threat categories described in the ENISA publication. No per-item URLs.

examples.note: Adversarial evasion attacks on ML models
Training data or model poisoning attacks
Attacks aimed at extracting or stealing ML models

data.official_name: Multilayer Framework for Good Cybersecurity Practices for AI
data.description: Multilayer framework providing cybersecurity practices for AI systems, addressing data integrity, model security, and operational concerns.
data.notes: Published as a formal ENISA guidance document. Covers cybersecurity practices across multiple layers of AI systems. Categories are described by slug names — no formal per-item identifiers.

versions_available.note: Initial publication of the multilayer AI cybersecurity framework.

children.description: AI framework practice category slug (lowercase hyphenated)

data.note: Conceptual taxonomy — cybersecurity practice categories described in the ENISA framework. No per-item URLs.

examples.note: Practices for ensuring AI training and operational data integrity
Practices for securing AI models against attacks and tampering
